templates.name: ai-playground
templates.description:
About the AI playground template

Generating innovative product ideas shouldn't take weeks of scattered brainstorming sessions. Yet most teams waste hours juggling disconnected tools, organizing sticky notes, and scheduling yet another meeting to "align on next steps." By the time you've documented concepts, stakeholders are asking about progress and your best ideas have lost momentum.

The AI Playground template teaches you a faster way. This interactive guide demonstrates how Miro's AI capabilities transform ideation from time-consuming chaos into structured, productive sessions. You'll learn to generate diverse concepts in minutes using AI Sidekicks, organize ideas visually with your team on Miro's innovation workspace, and convert rough thinking into polished deliverables—all without switching tools.

Unlike typical tutorials that explain features abstractly, this playground has you create actual product concepts while building AI confidence. The template provides step-by-step guidance through AI-assisted workflows: idea generation, collaborative refinement, concept selection, automated documentation, and format conversion. You finish with real outputs you can present to stakeholders, not just theoretical knowledge.

The structured five-step process works for solo exploration or team workshops. It supports both real-time collaboration and async work, making it ideal for distributed teams across time zones. Most importantly, the framework you learn here scales to real projects—teams use this approach for quarterly planning, feature prioritization, and innovation workshops.

How to use Miro's AI playground template

Here are five steps to master AI-powered product development using the playground template:

1. Set clear objectives for the session

Start by identifying your goal. The template includes example scenarios, but you'll get more value bringing an actual product challenge from your work. Ask yourself: What product opportunity are we exploring? Which customer problem are we solving? What decision does this concept need to inform?

Having a specific question generates better AI responses and keeps your session focused. "What features would help enterprise customers migrate faster?" works better than "Let's brainstorm product ideas."

2. Generate ideas with AI Sidekicks

Describe your challenge to Miro AI and watch it generate multiple concept directions instantly. Instead of staring at blank boards wondering where to start, you'll have rich material that sparks discussion and builds creative confidence.

This step typically produces more diverse ideas in 10 minutes than traditional brainstorming generates in an hour. The AI doesn't replace your expertise—it accelerates the initial generation so your team can focus on strategic refinement.

3. Collaborate and expand on concepts

Bring your team into the process. Add your own ideas alongside AI-generated concepts, use voting to identify favorites, and apply reactions to build consensus. Miro's visual workspace keeps everyone aligned whether they're contributing in real-time or asynchronously.

This collaborative layer is where human judgment shines. AI provides breadth, but your team adds depth—identifying which concepts align with your strategy, spotting opportunities the AI missed, and connecting ideas in novel ways.

4. Select and transform your strongest concepts

Review ideas together and identify the concepts with real potential. Use Miro's clustering features to group similar ideas and spot patterns. Then activate AI Flows to transform selected concepts into comprehensive product descriptions.

What normally takes days of documentation happens in minutes. AI Flows generate complete concept packages with descriptions, key features, target users, and potential challenges—formatted professionally and ready for stakeholder review.

5. Extend with AI-powered formats

Take your concepts further by converting them into additional deliverables. Need a development timeline? Transform your concept instantly. Want to map technical architecture? Generate a diagram. Planning feature prioritization? Create a table.

Miro's AI adapts your work to any stakeholder need without copy-pasting between tools. Everything stays connected on one canvas, maintaining context throughout your workflow.

Pro tips for success:

Experiment with different prompts to see how specificity affects output quality. Take notes on what works—which prompts generated useful responses, where you needed more human input. These insights help you adapt the approach to your team's needs.

Most importantly, end your session by deciding next steps. Schedule stakeholder reviews, assign owners for further development, or identify what additional research you need. The template accelerates ideation, but you still need to execute on the concepts you create.

What should be included in an AI playground template?

1. Specific starting objectives

The most productive sessions begin with clear questions. Vague prompts like "innovate our product" generate generic responses. Specific challenges like "reduce onboarding time for technical users" drive useful concepts and focused discussions.

2. Collaborative input

AI tools work best when combined with human perspective. Include time for team members to add their own ideas, vote on concepts, and refine AI-generated content. This balance between AI speed and human judgment produces the strongest outcomes.

3. Real project application

While structured as a learning experience, treat the session as productive work time. Many teams use the playground framework for actual planning sessions—quarterly roadmaps, feature prioritization, positioning exercises. The concepts you create can become real initiatives.

4. Iterative refinement

Your first AI-generated concepts won't be perfect. Great sessions treat outputs as starting points. Regenerate content with adjusted prompts, combine ideas in novel ways, and refine direction based on team discussion. This iteration is where breakthrough concepts emerge.

5. Follow-through planning

Always end by clarifying next steps. Which concepts need stakeholder input? Who owns further development? What research would validate these directions? The template compresses ideation time, but execution still requires your strategic planning.

Discover more product development templates to extend your AI-powered workflow.

Benefits of using the AI playground template

Learn by creating real deliverables

Unlike abstract tutorials, you finish with tangible product concepts you can develop further. This hands-on approach builds confidence faster than reading documentation—you experience exactly how AI accelerates your work.

Compress weeks into focused sessions

Generate more diverse ideas in 30 minutes than multi-hour traditional brainstorming produces. The structured process maintains quality while dramatically increasing speed, so you spend less time in ideation and more time building.

Build repeatable workflows

The five-step framework isn't just for practice. Teams adapt this approach for roadmap planning, innovation workshops, and strategic exercises. Once you understand the pattern, you can apply it across different project types and team needs.

Enable better async collaboration

Distributed teams struggle with brainstorming across time zones. AI-generated starting points give everyone a foundation to build from, making async contributions more substantive than "let's schedule another meeting to discuss."

Work seamlessly across formats

Stop switching between tools and manually reformatting information. Miro keeps everything connected on one canvas—from initial brainstorm to concept documentation to development timeline. This continuity reduces administrative overhead and maintains context.

Gain confidence with AI tools

Many teams feel uncertain about when and how to use AI in creative work. Guided practice removes this hesitation. You'll understand AI's strengths, learn where human judgment matters most, and develop intuition about effective prompts.

FAQ about the AI playground template

How do I use the AI playground template?

Open the template in your free Miro account and follow the built-in guide. Start with a specific product challenge, generate ideas using AI Sidekicks, collaborate with your team, select promising concepts, and transform them into complete deliverables. The template provides instructions at each step, so you're never wondering what comes next.

What are the benefits of customer journey mapping?

The AI playground template helps you compress ideation cycles from weeks to hours. You'll generate more diverse concepts faster, learn practical AI workflows you can reuse, and create presentation-ready deliverables without switching tools. It works for solo exploration or team workshops and supports both real-time and async collaboration.

Do I need AI experience to use this template?

Not at all. The template is specifically designed for first-time AI users. It provides step-by-step guidance that teaches effective AI workflows as you create actual product concepts.

How long does it take to complete the playground?

Individual exploration typically takes 30-45 minutes. Team workshops run 60-90 minutes, allowing time for collaborative discussion and refinement. The flexible format lets you move at your own pace.

Can I use the playground for real projects?

Absolutely. While framed as a learning experience, the outputs you create are production-ready product concepts. Many teams use this framework for actual planning sessions, quarterly roadmaps, and feature prioritization exercises.

What AI capabilities will I learn?

You'll gain hands-on experience with AI Sidekicks for idea generation, AI Flows for content development, and format conversion for creating timelines, diagrams, and tables. The template covers Miro's core AI features in practical context.

Does the template work for non-product use cases?

Yes. While framed around product development, the ideation and concept development workflow applies to marketing campaigns, process improvement initiatives, service design, and strategic planning. The structure adapts to different content types.

Can remote teams use this template effectively?

The template works beautifully for distributed teams. AI-generated starting points give everyone a foundation to build from, making async contributions more substantive. Team members can add ideas, vote, and refine concepts on their own schedule.

How is this different from just using ChatGPT?

Miro AI integrates ideation with visualization, collaboration, and format conversion on a single canvas. You're not just getting text responses—you're building visual workspaces that teams can contribute to in real-time while transforming concepts into multiple formats without copy-pasting between tools.

templates.name: user-feedback-to-insights
templates.description:
About the User Feedback to Insights template

User feedback is a gift, but it often arrives as a messy, overwhelming collection of notes, interview transcripts, and one-off comments. The User Feedback to Insights template provides a clear, structured process to find the signal in the noise. It helps your team move from a mountain of raw data to a prioritized list of actionable insights, all within Miro’s innovation workspace.

How to use the User Feedback to Insights template

- Gather your unstructured feedback. Start by adding all your raw user feedback to the board. This can include interview transcripts, customer support tickets, screenshots, or notes from user research sessions.
- Structure the feedback. Group similar pieces of feedback to identify recurring issues. The template provides a table to systematically capture each problem, its context, and its frequency, turning qualitative data into an organized format.
- Synthesize your key insights. With your feedback structured, you can now generate a summary document. This final step highlights the most critical user problems, recommends focus areas, and outlines the next steps.

Why use the User Feedback to Insights template?

- Make sense of messy data. Go from a scattered collection of notes to a clear, synthesized overview of your users' biggest pain points.
- Create clear team alignment. The visual workflow and final summary ensure everyone understands which problems are the highest priority and why.

Accelerate your workflow. By structuring the synthesis process, your team can move from feedback to action faster, creating a direct input for your product roadmap.

templates.name: ai-sprint-planning-template
templates.description:
How to use the AI Sprint Planning template

- Review the sprint backlog. Begin by gathering your team to discuss items currently in progress and review the highest-priority items from your product backlog. You can import your Jira tickets directly onto the board to keep everything in one place.
- Plan the sprint scope. As a team, decide which tasks to tackle in the next sprint. Drag cards from the backlog into the ‘Next Sprint’ column and use story points to discuss effort, complexity, and team capacity.
- Define next steps. Once you’ve agreed on the sprint’s scope, identify any remaining actions the team needs to take. Use sticky notes to capture tasks like scheduling follow-up meetings or clarifying dependencies, ensuring nothing is missed.

templates.name: daily-stand-up-meeting-ai-template

- Increase visibility and team alignment. The visual Kanban board provides a shared source of truth for daily progress. The AI-generated summary ensures alignment continues even after the meeting ends.
- Solve problems before they slow you down. By visualizing blockers in a dedicated lane every day, your team can address issues proactively and maintain sprint momentum.
- Strengthen team connection. The built-in temperature check provides a moment for the team to connect on a human level, fostering a more supportive and aware environment.

templates.name: ai-prototype-template
templates.description:
How to use the AI Accelerated Prototype template

- Select the Prototype & Describe Your Vision. Start by selecting the prototype in the Design Prototype Sidekick. Then, write a simple text prompt for the screen you want to create. You can choose a quick prompt or write your own, from "a login screen for a modern travel app" to "a user profile page with a photo and recent activity."

- Refine and Iterate. Chat with the AI to refine your design. Ask it to change colors, add elements, or adjust the layout until it matches your vision.
- Apply and Build Your User Flow. Apply the finished design to the canvas. Repeat the process to create all the screens for your application. You can then connect them to build a complete, interactive prototype that demonstrates the entire user journey, ready for you to edit, share, and continue creating.

About the From user flow to prototype AI Template

A user flow diagram is a great start, but it's still just a diagram. The real test comes when you turn those abstract steps into tangible screens. The From user flow to prototype AI-accelerated template closes that gap instantly. It takes your user flow diagram and automatically generates a multi-screen prototype, turning your team's structural thinking into a visual, interactive experience you can test and share.

How to use the From user flow to prototype AI template

- Add your user flow diagram. Place a diagram on the board that outlines the steps of your user journey. The more detail in the diagram, the better the result.
- Provide a style reference (optional). Add a screenshot of your existing product or a style guide image. The prototype will adopt the look and feel from this image.
- Run the generator. With your diagram and style reference in place, simply run the tool to begin the generation.
- Review your prototype. The template will generate a series of connected screens based on your flow, ready for you to review, test, and share.

templates.name: ai-entity-relationship-diagram-template
templates.description:
How to use the AI Entity Relationship Diagram Template

- Write your prompt. Start by writing a clear, descriptive prompt that outlines the entities, attributes, and relationships of your database schema. Choose 'ERD' as the diagram type.
- Run the AI flow. With your prompt ready, hit run to execute the AI flow. The template will automatically generate both an ERD on the canvas and a structured technical document.
- Iterate and refine. Review the generated diagram and document. Iterate on the prompt for the best results. If you need changes, you can adjust your prompt and run the flow again, or edit the diagram elements directly on the board to add colors and styling. For changes to the document, iterate on the prompt and click run on the doc.
- Collaborate with your team. Share the board with your team to review the design, gather feedback, and ensure everyone is aligned on the database structure before implementation.

templates.name: ai-pitch-deck-generator
templates.description:
About the AI Pitch deck generator Template

That brilliant idea in your head needs a compelling story to make it a reality. But turning a concept into a persuasive pitch deck is a challenge. The Pitch deck generator template simplifies the entire process, providing a structured path from initial thought to a polished, presentation-ready deck. Whether you want to leverage AI to create a deck in seconds or build it collaboratively with your team, this template is your starting point for crafting a narrative that gets results.

How to use the AI Pitch deck generator template

- Capture your core idea. Start with the simple "Pitch draft" document. Answering the prompts helps you clarify the problem, your solution, the opportunity, and the potential impact. This brief becomes the solid foundation for your entire pitch.
- Choose your creation path. You have two options. You can use Miro AI to instantly generate a full presentation from your brief, or you can manually populate the comprehensive set of slides included in the template.
- Refine your narrative. Whether you start with an AI-generated draft or build the deck slide-by-slide, your team can collaborate in real-time to refine the content, adjust the visuals, and perfect the story. The template includes slides for everything from the problem and solution to your business model, metrics, and team.

templates.name: visualize-product-concepts
templates.description:
About the Visualize Product Concepts Template

The Visualize Product Concepts template acts as your AI-powered art director, turning simple product shots into full-blown creative concepts. It’s a practical tool for marketers, designers, and brand strategists to explore and present visual ideas quickly, bridging the gap between a product and how it will be perceived in the world.

How to use the Visualize Product Concepts template

- Add your product photo. Start with a clear image of your product. This will be the subject of your new, generated concept.
- Provide style references. Drop in images that capture the mood, color palette, and texture you're aiming for.
- Set the scene (optional). If you have a specific background in mind, add a location image.
- Generate your concept. With your inputs in place, run the flow to generate a new, ready-to-use image that brings your creative vision to life.

Why use the Visualize Product Concepts template?

- Explore creative ideas instantly. Move beyond words and generate tangible visual concepts in minutes. Share them with your team to get feedback and build alignment early in the creative process.
- Reduce production costs and time. Create professional-grade concept images without the expense and logistical challenges of a full photoshoot or complex CGI work.
- Align your team on a visual direction. Use generated images to create a shared, concrete understanding of a brand's look and feel, ensuring your team is working toward the same goal.

templates.name: ai-product-brief-brainstorm-template
templates.description:
How to use the AI Accelerated Product Brief Brainstorm template

- Define the problem. Start with the most important question: what user problem or business opportunity are we trying to solve? Get alignment on the "why" before you discuss the "what."
- Identify the audience. Who are we building this for? This section helps your team focus on the specific user segment, their needs, and their challenges.
- Establish success metrics. How will we know if we've succeeded? Define clear and measurable KPIs that connect the feature directly to business outcomes.
- Brainstorm potential solutions. With the problem, audience, and metrics clearly defined, your team can now generate focused solutions that are already grounded in strategy.

templates.name: ai-4-l-s-retrospective-template
templates.description:
About the AI 4 L's Retrospective Template

After a sprint ends, it’s easy to jump to the next project without pausing to reflect. The AI accelerated 4 L's Retrospective template offers a simple but effective framework to make sure that learning happens. It guides your team to look at your work from four different angles: what you liked, what you learned, what you lacked, and what you longed for. This method encourages a balanced conversation, focusing not just on challenges but also on celebrating successes and acknowledging new discoveries.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: ai-image-style-mixer
templates.description:
About the AI Image Style Mixer template

Translating an artistic style from an idea into a finished image can be a challenge. The AI Image Style Transfer template makes that process visual and concrete. In Miro's innovation workspace, you can use this simple framework to explore creative directions, define a clear aesthetic, and generate new visual concepts by combining the style of reference images with your own photos.

How to use the AI Image Style Mixer template

- Gather your style references. Find one to three images that capture the look and feel you want to achieve. Add them to the Style References 🎨 frame.
- Add your photo. Place the image you want to restyle into the Your Photo 📸 frame.
- Generate your new image. Use the template to transfer the style from your references to your photo, creating a new, stylized version in the Target Image frame.
- Review and align. With the original photo, references, and output all in one place, you can easily discuss the results with your team and align on a creative direction.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: world-creator-moodboard
templates.description:
About the World Creator Moodboard Template

Every great world, whether for a game, film, or story, begins as a collection of scattered ideas, images, and "what-ifs." The World Creator Moodboard template provides a space on Miro's innovation workspace to bring those fragments together. It’s designed to transform a simple moodboard of inspiration into a structured, high-level concept pitch, giving your creative project a solid and exciting starting point.

How to use the World Creator Moodboard template

- Drop in your inspiration. Start by gathering your initial ideas. This can be anything—images that set a mood, sticky notes with character concepts, or keywords for a setting, such as 'treehouse kingdom' in a 'jungle setting'. Add everything that inspires you to the first frame.
- Run the flow. With your inspiration gathered, simply hit "Run." The template's automated flow reads your inputs and begins to build out a structured concept, turning your abstract ideas into a coherent vision that can include game ideas, visuals, and even video snippets.
- Review your world concept. The flow generates a detailed pitch, including vivid descriptions of settings and characters, core themes, and visual elements that bring your world to life. This becomes the foundation for your project.
- Make it your own. The generated concept is just the beginning. From here, you can add new documents, generate more visuals like character designs, and spin up new storylines. It’s a launchpad for your imagination.

Why use the World Creator Moodboard template?

- Go from abstract to tangible, fast. The template provides the structure needed to quickly shape raw inspiration into a concrete plan. It helps you overcome the dreaded "blank page" and build creative momentum.
- Spark new creative directions. By automatically generating connections and ideas from your initial input, the template can uncover surprising themes and narrative paths you might not have considered, pushing your concept even further.
- Build an aligned foundation for your project. Use the generated concept as a central source of truth for your team. Whether you're a game designer, writer, or filmmaker, everyone can start from the same shared vision, ensuring alignment from day one.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: multi-agent-boardroom-review-flow
templates.description:
The Multi-Agent Boardroom: Automated Strategic Review

Turn a single vision into a full-scale executive critique in minutes. This template uses a multi-LLM "Flow" to simulate a high-level leadership team.



How it works:

- Phase 1: Analysis. A Growth CRO (GPT-5), Risk Council (Claude 4.5), and Ruthless COO (Gemini 2.5) evaluate your strategy.
- Phase 2: The Critique. Agents cross-examine and challenge each other’s logic.
- Phase 3: The Verdict. A Chairman consolidates insights into a final plan, timeline, and presentation.
templates.author: Tomás Dostal-Freire

templates.name: low-fidelity-wireframe-ai-template
templates.description:
How to use the AI Accelerated Low Fidelity Wireframe template

- Start with a simple prompt. Open the template and use the chat to tell Sidekick (Miro’s AI) what you want to create. You can choose a quick prompt or write your own, like "a mobile app dashboard with a bottom navigation bar" or "a website homepage with a hero image and a three-column feature section."
- Generate your wireframe instantly. The AI will interpret your prompt and generate a low-fidelity wireframe directly on the canvas, using standard UI components to build the layout.
- Refine and iterate with AI. Once the initial wireframe is created, you can continue to give Sidekick instructions to refine it. Ask it to "add a search bar to the header" or "change the card layout to a list view" and watch it update in real-time.
- Apply to canvas, collaborate, and build. Apply the generated wireframe to the canvas to edit and share. With your foundational layout ready, you and your team can begin to collaborate. Add sticky notes for feedback, leave comments for discussion, and start building out the details of your design.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: ai-requirements-gathering-template
templates.description:
How to use the AI Requirements Gathering template

- Brainstorm your requirements. Start by getting every idea, feature request, and user need onto the board as a sticky note. In this step, quantity is more important than quality.
- Prioritize using the MoSCoW method. Move each sticky note into one of the four quadrants: Must-haves (critical for launch), Should-haves (important but not vital), Could-haves (desirable but can be postponed), and Nice-to-haves (extras).
- Synthesize into a PRD. With your requirements clearly categorized, you now have the essential input needed. Use this prioritized list to generate a formal Product Requirements Document (PRD) that outlines the project's purpose, features, and success criteria.
templates.author: Miro

Move beyond a standard corporate headshot and show your team who you are, both inside and outside of work.

Personalized avatars can act as conversation starters and help build a stronger sense of community, especially in remote and distributed teams.
- Make AI fun and accessible.



The template demystifies the process of AI image generation, providing a structured and playful way to create something unique without needing any technical expertise.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: pricing-page-analysis
templates.description:
Drop a screenshot of your pricing page to get a full analysis on what works and what needs more optimization!

By combining visual analysis, structured prompts, and guided worksheets, this template gives founders, product teams, and growth teams a reliable way to understand what works and what does not. It helps establish a consistent review process that supports better pricing decisions, clearer customer communication, and stronger monetization strategies.

Template Features

- Tier Mapping Framework: Break down your pricing tiers into clear categories such as Free, Standard, and Premium to compare structure, clarity, and perceived value.
- Screenshot Integration: Add a screenshot of your live pricing page directly into the board to analyze layout, hierarchy, and messaging in context.
- Feature Comparison Grid: Review how features are distributed across pricing levels and identify whether your value differentiation is clear and compelling.
- Value Communication Review: Analyze how benefits, outcomes, and differentiators are presented and whether customers can easily interpret what they get at each tier.
- Competitive Awareness Prompts: Worksheets help you compare your page structure to industry expectations and identify whether your model aligns with customer mental models.
- Actionable Improvement Areas: Structured sections help you capture insights, define changes, and align on what updates will improve conversions and customer clarity.

templates.author: Leah Tharin

templates.name: presentation-ai-template
templates.description:
How to use the AI Presentation template

- Outline your narrative. Start by using the Table of Contents / Agenda and Section Title slides to structure the flow of your story. This helps you think through your key points before diving into the details.
- Flesh out your content. Fill in the pre-designed slides with your key messages, data, and insights. The template includes a variety of layouts for showcasing content, such as:

- Feature slides to introduce groundbreaking features.
- A Gantt Timeline slide to visualize project schedules.
- Data charts and diagrams for market analysis (Total, Serviceable, and Obtainable Market).
- Frameworks like the Matrix Slide and Alignment Scale.
- A dedicated Meet the team slide.
- Add your visual elements. Customize the look and feel by adding images, embedding media (GIFs, videos, websites), or using Miro's built-in styling options. To brand your deck, click the 'More' dots in the Slide context menu, select 'Styles', and apply your brand style or choose from a preset.
- Collaborate and refine. Share the presentation with your team for real-time or async feedback directly on the board using the Feedback Slide.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: activity-check-in-icebreaker-ai
templates.description:
About the AI Check-in Icebreaker template

Routine check-ins can often feel flat, with team members giving the same one-word answers meeting after meeting. The Check-in Icebreaker template transforms this ritual into a moment of creative connection by using AI to translate feelings into unique visuals. It gives your team a fresh, engaging way to share how they’re doing without having to find the perfect words.
This template turns Miro’s innovation workspace into a gallery of team sentiment, providing a fun and insightful way to kick off a collaborative session. It’s designed to help your team connect on a more human level before diving into the work.

How to use the Check-in Icebreaker template

- Choose an emotion. Start by using the built-in feelings wheel to identify what you’re feeling in the moment. This helps you pinpoint a specific emotion to work with.
- Generate your visual. Fill in the blanks in the pre-written text prompt with your chosen emotion to create your state card. You can modify the prompt as needed by editing the text directly in the field above the image placeholder. Miro AI will then generate a unique image that represents your current state.
- Share and discuss. Once everyone has their image, take turns sharing them with the team. Share reflections with one another, explaining what your image means to you and how it reflects your state of mind.
- Capture key insights. Use the dedicated "Capture insights" space to jot down important reflections, action items, or team takeaways that emerge from the conversation.

Why use the Check-in Icebreaker template?

- Encourage authentic sharing. The visual prompts help team members go beyond surface-level updates like "I'm fine," allowing for more nuanced and honest conversations.
- Spark creative connection. By discussing personal and unique images, your team can connect in a more creative and memorable way, fostering a stronger sense of psychological safety.
- Make check-ins more engaging. This template turns a routine process into a fun and interactive activity that everyone can look forward to, boosting energy at the start of a meeting.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: simplified-design-thinking-process
templates.description:
Welcome. Thanks for being interested in checking out the Design Thinking process through this template. I've simplified and modified this process to make it easier and more accessible to all. We often talk about product-market fit, yet we have to remember that users are the heart of our products. The more we learn, think about, and empathize with users, the better our product will be and the more marketable it becomes. We need to think about user-product-market fit deeply. And as we understand the user, we also benefit from thinking about the general commercial landscape, which this template also helps with. Whether you’re on a small team, at a startup, or just prototyping to find user-product-market fit and build an MVP, you may or may not have a well-developed Product or Service organization. If you aren’t there yet, this template is for you! You might be a BA/Product Owner, a Delivery Coach/Product Owner, a CTO/Engineering Manager/Lead Dev/Product Owner or even a solo vibe coder.



In this template you'll be chatting & interacting with a team of expert AI teammates to help guide you. You'll even attend virtual meetings with them to discuss and refine your work. This template is an exploratory, conversational, learning, and reflection experience that will guide you & your team from zero to actionable, refined and prioritized user stories that can go right into your JIRA or Azure. It can also sync with your Trello. Along the way, it produces well-structured documents that you have discussed, improved, and vetted. Then you can use and share with stakeholders, actual customers, and your own human team. No AI slop enters your workstream unreviewed, because you can constantly review and rework yourself or with the AI team to ensure the work meets your needs and goals. Even with Miro's new and transformative AI enablement, the key is to slow down, review, re-read and re-think together. Be prepared to build the user stories you develop with this template as experiments, test them with users, and never hesitate to pivot based on user feedback. Feedback is your gold. No one gets it right the first time; everyone's doing their best to make a series of wise bets and learn fast. Learning and pivoting isn't failure. It's how the process works.



You can return to the template and go back through the process to refine your work towards better experiments, better bets, as often as you need. Best of luck developing your product or service idea. Enjoy!
templates.author: Fortune Buchholtz

templates.name: pi-planning-ai-template
templates.description:
How to use the AI Accelerated PI Planning template

- Define the Product Increment Scope. Begin by aligning all teams on the high-level business context. Clarify priorities, intended outcomes, and owners to establish a shared understanding of the goals. Agree on an estimation approach (e.g., story points) and the iteration cadence for the PI before breaking down the work.
- Break down the work by team. Each team pulls their assigned features or epics into their dedicated area on the board. From there, they decompose the work into smaller user stories and tasks, sequencing them into specific iteration timelines while capturing team-level risks and assumptions.
- Conduct an Agile Release Train Review. With each team’s plan visualized, use the dependencies app to connect related work between teams, which works with imported Jira cards. Conduct a final review with the entire ART to walk through the consolidated plan, address risks, and secure commitment. You can even let Miro AI help summarize and assess risks and dependencies.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: impact-effort-matrix-to-table
templates.description:
About the Impact-Effort Matrix to Table template

Your team has plenty of ideas, but deciding where to focus can be a challenge. The Impact-Effort Matrix to Table template offers a visual and collaborative workspace to move from a sea of possibilities to a clear, prioritized action plan. It’s designed to help your team make objective decisions together, turning a dynamic prioritization exercise into a structured list of tasks, instantly.

How to use the Impact-Effort Matrix to Table template

- Brainstorm your tasks. Add all your potential tasks, features, or ideas to the board as sticky notes.
- Run your impact-effort exercise. As a team, drag each sticky note onto the matrix, discussing where it falls in terms of potential impact and the effort required to complete it. Use dot voting to capture team excitement for each task.
- Generate your task list. The template automatically converts the stickies from the visual matrix into a clean, organized table, capturing the impact, effort, and vote count for each item.
- Create your action plan. With your tasks now neatly sorted and prioritized, you have a clear, actionable backlog to guide your team’s next steps.

Why use the Impact-Effort Matrix to Table template?

- Make objective decisions as a team. Move past debate and opinion by using a shared framework to evaluate priorities visually and collaboratively.
- Go from visual exploration to action instantly. The template does the work of translating your messy, creative brainstorming session into a structured, organized backlog that’s ready for action.
- Align your plan with team energy. By incorporating dot voting, you can see which tasks have the most team excitement and factor that energy into your prioritization plan.

templates.author: Jesse Greenhouse

templates.name: ai-aws-cloud-infrastructure-optimization-template
templates.board_name: AI AWS Cloud Infrastructure Optimization Template
templates.description:
About the AI AWS Cloud Infrastructure Optimization template

Managing a complex AWS environment can feel like navigating a maze in the dark. It’s hard to see the full picture, even harder to spot where you’re wasting money, and nearly impossible to get everyone aligned on the path forward. The AWS Cloud Infrastructure Optimization template brings your entire architecture into the light, providing a visual workspace for your team to collaboratively analyze, improve, and plan. It turns abstract infrastructure into a concrete, shared diagram that everyone can understand and act on.

How to use the AI AWS Cloud Infrastructure Optimization template

- Visualize your current architecture. Start by importing your existing AWS infrastructure directly into Miro using the AWS Cloud View import tool. This creates an up-to-date diagram of your current setup, giving your team a single source of truth to work from.
- Analyze for improvements. Use the integrated AI tools, like the AWS Well Architected Sidekick, to run an analysis based on the AWS Well-Architected Framework. The AI will suggest specific problems and opportunities related to cost, security, performance, and reliability.
- Design your target architecture. Based on the AI-driven insights, collaborate with your team on the canvas to design an improved target architecture. Drag and drop services, model changes, and see the impact in real time.
- Optimize your costs. Once you have a target architecture, use the AWS Cost Calculator to understand the financial impact of your proposed changes. This allows you to make data-backed decisions that balance performance with budget.
- Document and share your plan. With your optimized architecture finalized, use the built-in documentation features to capture your plan, decisions, and next steps, ensuring stakeholders are fully aligned.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: ai-brand-voice-guidelines
templates.description:
How to use the AI Accelerated Brand Voice Guidelines template

- Define your brand foundation. This is the heart of your brand's identity. Work with your team to capture your core mission and values (e.g., Customer Obsession, Social Responsibility, Financial Empowerment), define the key personality traits that bring your brand to life (e.g., Empathetic, Optimistic, Playful), and get specific about the target audience you're speaking to (e.g., Young Professionals & Students, Tech-Savvy & Mobile-First, Urban Dwellers).
- Generate your voice guidelines. Use the foundation you've built to create practical rules for writing. For each personality trait, brainstorm what it sounds like in practice. This section turns your abstract ideas into a functional guide that anyone on your team can use to write clear, consistent copy.

Why use this template?

- Create a consistent brand experience. Ensure that your brand sounds the same everywhere, from your website and marketing campaigns to your product interface and customer support interactions.
- Scale your content creation. As your team grows, new writers can quickly learn your brand voice, allowing you to maintain quality and consistency without slowing down.
- Build cross-functional alignment. This collaborative exercise ensures that product, marketing, and design teams all share the same understanding of how the brand should communicate with customers.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: uml-sequence-diagram-ai-template
templates.description:
Why use the AI Accelerated UML Sequence Diagram template?

- Create diagrams in seconds, not hours. Move from a complex idea to a clear sequence diagram with a simple text prompt. Let AI do the heavy lifting of initial creation so you can focus on refining the details.
- Keep your documentation in sync. Bridge the gap between visual models and written specs. Because your document is generated from your diagram, you can ensure your technical documentation is an accurate reflection of your system's design.
- Improve team clarity and alignment. Use a clear, visual diagram as the single source of truth for how a system works. Reduce ambiguity and ensure everyone from engineering to product management is aligned on the technical details.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: ai-reverse-brainstorming-template
templates.description:
How to use the AI Accelerated Reverse Brainstorming template

- Define your goal. Start with a clear objective for your project or feature. What does success look like? For example, your goal might be to "Increase NPS" or "Decrease the percentage of customers who cancel their service."
- Flip the goal. Turn your success statement into a failure scenario. For example, if your goal is "Increase NPS," the flipped goal becomes "Worsen NPS."
- Brainstorm causes of failure. With the failure scenario in mind, your team can now list all the potential reasons it could happen. This could range from major issues like removing customer support and not fixing bugs to confusing copy or poor performance.
- Generate solutions and plan your actions. For each potential cause of failure, brainstorm a preventative solution. This turns a list of abstract risks into a concrete action plan, complete with an owner and due date for each task, ensuring your team can build a more resilient and successful product.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: test-case-generator-qa
templates.description:
About the QA Test Case Generator template

Tired of the slow, manual process of writing test cases? It can be a bottleneck that delays development and leaves room for human error. The QA Test Case Generator template is your solution to this challenge. It’s designed to help you increase both the speed and quality of your testing workflow. Stop writing test plans from scratch and start automating the heavy lifting, so your team can focus on what matters most: building a great product.

How to use the QA Test Case Generator template

Get started in just a few simple steps.

- Add your requirement: Drop a user story, acceptance criteria, or any detailed functional requirement directly into the template frame.
- Generate test cases: Run the tool to automatically generate a complete test plan based on your input.
- Review the output: The template produces a comprehensive table of functional, negative, and boundary test cases, complete with steps to reproduce and expected results.
- Start testing: Use the generated table as a clear and actionable checklist for your entire QA process.

Why use the QA Test Case Generator template?

Adopt this template to transform your team's efficiency and effectiveness.

- Accelerate your QA cycle: By automating test case creation, you dramatically reduce the time spent on planning. This allows your team to move from feature requirements to active testing much faster.
- Improve test coverage: The generator systematically identifies edge cases that might otherwise be overlooked. It creates a robust suite of positive, negative, and boundary tests to ensure your features are resilient and reliable.
- Foster team alignment: Keep your development and QA teams on the same page. With the user story and its corresponding test cases living in the same shared workspace, everyone has a single source of truth, leading to better collaboration and clearer communication.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: team-agreement-rituals-workshop
templates.description:
Team agreement & rituals workshop helps the team to speed up the process of forming commitments to actually living them every day through rituals, experiments & ways of working. It is enriched with an AI sidekick called Psychological safety coach & prepped up with two Flows. 

Team agreement

Plan about 45 minutes to complete the first frame.
Agree on the focus areas with your team before the workshop.
Give everyone 3–5 minutes per area to write down their agreements.
Once that’s done, read them aloud, group similar ones together if needed, and vote using dots or the voting tool.
When you’re ready, just press Run — the team agreement will be generated automatically.



Team rituals

Plan about 20 minutes for the second frame. 
If the team is already working together, keep the Continue frame; otherwise, remove it before the workshop. 
Give everyone 3–5 minutes to write ideas on how you can live your team agreement day to day. 
Read them aloud, group similar ones if needed, then vote using dots or the voting tool. 
Move the winning items into the box labeled Voted ideas. 
When you’re ready, press Run—you’ll get a clear step-by-step implementation for the voted ideas.
templates.author: Switch to Eleven

templates.name: workflow-prototyping-template
templates.description:
The Workflow Prototyping Template by Dexter Zhuang is a structured, end to end workspace that helps product teams turn user research into clear workflows and actionable prototypes. It guides teams from raw qualitative insights to visual workflow diagrams and prototype solutions, all in one collaborative Miro board.

Designed for teams that want to move faster without skipping discovery, this template combines research synthesis, workflow mapping, and prototyping into a single, repeatable process. It is especially useful for uncovering friction in real user journeys and designing solutions that directly address those gaps.

By using AI assisted prompts alongside visual collaboration, teams can align faster, reduce ambiguity, and create prototypes grounded in real user behavior rather than assumptions.

Template Features

- Research Insights Zone: Capture and organize user evidence such as pain points, key quotes, observed behaviors, current tools, workarounds, and user goals. Built to help teams focus on what users actually do, not just what they say.
- AI Assisted Insight Synthesis: Use structured prompts to summarize research into clear themes, connect patterns across insights, and surface high impact opportunities that feed directly into workflow design.
- Workflow Diagram Mapping: Translate summarized insights into step by step workflows using flowcharts or Mermaid style diagrams. Highlight bottlenecks, delays, handoffs, and high friction moments in the current experience.
- Prototype Solutions Zone: Turn workflows into clickable or visual prototypes by generating UI screens directly from workflows and insights. Designed to reduce guesswork and keep prototypes tightly aligned to real problems.
- End to End Flow Structure: Clear step sequencing ensures each output builds on the previous one, from research to workflow to prototype, without losing context along the way.
- AI Friendly and Modular: Built to work seamlessly with Miro AI tools, allowing teams to mix prompts, documents, tables, diagrams, and prototypes based on their workflow needs.

Benefits

- Move from research to prototype faster without sacrificing rigor.
- Reveal hidden friction in user workflows and decision paths.
- Improve collaboration between product, design, and engineering teams.
- Create prototypes grounded in validated user insights.
- Establish a repeatable process for workflow driven product discovery.

Ideal For

- Product managers and product designers
- UX and service designers
- Research driven product teams
- Startups and scaleups building new flows or redesigning existing ones
- Teams adopting AI assisted product discovery and prototyping
templates.author: Dexter Zhuang

templates.name: responsive-mockups-from-desktop-to-mobile
templates.board_name: Responsive Mockups from Desktop to Mobile
templates.description:
About the Responsive Mockups from Desktop to Mobile template

Creating a design that looks great on a desktop is one thing, but how do you make sure it works just as well on a phone? The Responsive Mockups from Desktop to Mobile template helps you bridge that gap instantly. Instead of manually rebuilding your layouts for different screen sizes, this tool uses the power of AI to adapt your desktop prototype into a mobile version, right on your Miro board. It’s a smarter way for your team to stay ahead in a multi-device world.

How to use the Responsive Mockups from Desktop to Mobile template

- Add your desktop prototype. Start by placing your existing desktop design screen onto the Miro board. This will be the source for your mobile conversion.
- Run the AI conversion. With your desktop screen selected, simply activate the AI tool by selecting 'Run' with the example prompt. It will analyze the layout, content, and components of your design.
- Review your new mobile layout. The AI will generate a new mobile-sized prototype based on your desktop version. You can then review, refine, and use this new layout for further testing and development, ensuring your design is ready for any screen.
Tip: The more details you add to your prompt, the better your results will be.

Why use the Responsive Mockups from Desktop to Mobile template?

- Accelerate your design workflow. Stop wasting time manually recreating screens. Generate a mobile version in seconds and dedicate your team's energy to solving bigger user problems.
- Ensure a consistent cross-platform experience. By adapting directly from your desktop design, you can maintain visual and functional consistency, giving users a familiar experience on any device.

Facilitate early feedback and iteration. Quickly create mobile mockups to share with stakeholders or use in user testing. This makes it easier to gather feedback and make improvements long before the development phase begins.

templates.name: retro-insights-flow-for-small-businesses
templates.board_name: Retro Insights Flow for Small Businesses
templates.description: A lightweight retro template that helps small teams make better decisions every week. Add your notes, press run, and turn the messy middle into something you can act on. It’s the fastest way to turn loose conversations into clear insights, steady progress, and a weekly rhythm that actually moves your business forward.
templates.author: Cat G

templates.name: user-persona-generator
templates.description:
The User Persona Generator template guides you through a structured 3-step workflow to create accurate user personas based on real data. First, gather inputs across four key areas: research findings, user actions & pains, goals & motivations, and additional context. Then, use AI assistance to summarize this information into cohesive persona profiles. Finally, generate presentation-ready slides for each persona with key insights formatted for easy sharing.

This template transforms scattered user research into clear, actionable personas that your entire team can reference when making product decisions. By combining your research with AI assistance, you'll save hours of manual work while ensuring your personas accurately represent real users and their needs—helping you build products that truly solve customer problems.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: ai-product-requirements-prioritization
templates.description:
How to use the AI Accelerated Product Requirements Prioritization template

- Brainstorm your requirements. Get all the potential features, fixes, and ideas out of your team’s heads and onto the board. This is where you capture everything you could do for your product.
- Plot each requirement on the matrix. This is the core of the exercise. For each idea, discuss with your team where it falls on the grid based on its potential impact for the user and the effort required to build it.
- Generate a priority table. With your priorities visualized on the matrix, you can now create a clear, prioritized list of requirements. This creates a clear input for your product roadmap or backlog, backed by a shared understanding across the team.
templates.author: Miro

templates.name: odyssey-planning
templates.description:
Map out three distinct paths for your professional future using this strategic career planning template. Inspired by the Odyssey Planning exercise from Designing Your Life by Bill Burnett and Dave Evans, this framework helps you move beyond linear career thinking to explore multiple possibilities for your role and growth trajectory.

What you'll create:

Plan A captures your current trajectory—the natural progression if you continue building on where you are today. Plan B charts an alternative route you'd consider if your first path became unavailable or less appealing. Blue Sky removes all practical constraints—what direction would you explore if limitations weren't a factor.

For each path, you'll think about the immediate term and a few years out, rate your confidence and satisfaction levels, and identify the resources, skills, and conversations needed to move forward. This template uses Miro's AI-powered Flows to help you progress between steps.

Why this exercise matters:

Career growth rarely follows a predictable line. This template helps you show up to manager conversations with clarity about what you want, evaluate new opportunities against a broader picture of your goals, and make proactive choices about your next steps.

Whether you're seeking growth, considering a pivot, or simply want more clarity about your professional direction, Odyssey Planning helps you explore your career aspirations and frame them into concrete, actionable paths you can reflect on and refine over time.

Ideal for individual contributors, managers, and anyone navigating career transitions or growth conversations.

Tips and ideas for re-mixing:

- Bring in any context from other career development or insights you have for yourself, like manager and peer reviews and feedback, or DISC or 16 Personalities assessments.
- Connect this context to the Miro Flow and adjust the instructions so it can provide summaries tailored to you.

How do you move from idea to value?

This template simplifies your discovery journey, to use it:

templates.description:
What this template is about

- An interactive framework that helps writers practice creative writing skills by combining human creativity with AI assistance
- Features a structured workflow with theme selection, writing prompts, visual inspiration, and story twists to spark imagination

What this help you achieve

- Develop creative writing skills while using AI as a collaborative tool rather than relying on it to do all the writing

- Writers of all experience levels looking to practice and enhance their creative writing through guided exercises

templates.author: Daiana Kaplan

templates.name: miroverse-sidekick-image-generator
templates.description:
About the Miroverse Sidekick Image Generator template

As teams build custom AI assistants, giving them a unique identity is key to making them feel like a natural part of the workflow. The Create your Miroverse Sidekick avatar template offers a simple, visual framework to move from abstract ideas to a concrete visual. It helps your team design a memorable face for your custom AI, turning a powerful tool into a recognizable teammate.

How to use the Miroverse Sidekick Image Generator template

- Create your moodboard. Start by gathering images, color palettes, and design styles that capture the personality and function of your Sidekick. Add your favorite inspiration images, colors, and styles. This is a great step to collaborate on with your team to align on a creative direction.

Why use the Miroverse Sidekick Image Generator template?

- Give your AI a unique identity. Move beyond generic icons and create a memorable character that fits your team’s culture and the Sidekick's purpose.

A structured creative process. The template provides a clear path from idea to a finished asset, aligning the team and making the design process faster and more focused.

The template analyzes the colors in your image and gives you the exact data you need to apply them thoughtfully and consistently.

How to use the Color Palette Extractor template

It's a simple, four-step process to get your new color palette.

- Add an inspiration image. Find a picture with colors you love and drop it into the designated frame on the board.
- Watch the magic happen. The template automatically extracts the main colors and creates a visual palette and summary slide.
- Review your color details. A table populates with the color names, hex codes, suggested roles (like foreground, background, or accent), and accessibility notes.
- Apply your new palette. Use the provided information to apply colors consistently across your designs, from websites to presentations.

Why use the Color Palette Extractor template?

There are three big reasons to use this template for your next project.

- Get it done fast. Go from a reference image to a complete color system in just a few seconds. No more manual color picking or guesswork.
- Be objective. Base your design decisions on concrete data extracted from an image you already know works. This gives your color choices a clear and logical foundation.

templates.name: jobs-to-be-done-extractor
templates.description:
About the Jobs to be Done Generator template

Great products are built to solve real user problems. The Jobs to be Done (JTBD) Generator template gives your team a simple framework to uncover those problems. By focusing on the "job" a user is trying to get done, you can move past assumptions and define the actual motivation and desired outcome driving user behavior. This template helps you articulate the "why" behind your work.

How to use the Jobs to be Done Generator template

- Gather your inputs. Start by adding your research and context to the board. Define your user personas, capture their specific pain points and situations (e.g., "I am under pressure to hit end-of-quarter deadlines"), and list their desired outcomes.
- Synthesize into Job Stories. The template guides you to combine your inputs into the classic JTBD format: "When [situation], I want to [motivation], so I can [expected outcome]." This structure turns scattered ideas into a clear and actionable statement that describes the user's need.

Why use the Jobs to be Done Generator template?

- Focus on the problem, not the feature. Job stories ground your team in the user’s context, which ensures you are building the right thing to solve a genuine need.
- Create deep user empathy. By stepping into the user's shoes, your team develops a shared understanding of their frustrations and goals, making their challenges tangible.
- Build alignment across your team. Get product managers, designers, and engineers on the same page about the fundamental "why" before design or development begins.

templates.name: activity-impact-effort-matrix-eisenhower
templates.description:
About the Activity: Impact/Effort Matrix (Eisenhower) template

When every task feels like a top priority, it’s hard to know where to begin. The Impact/Effort Matrix (Eisenhower) template cuts through the noise with a simple, visual framework that helps your team make clear decisions about what to focus on. Based on the proven Urgent/Important Principle, this template provides a shared space for your team to sort tasks and align on a path forward in Miro’s collaborative innovation workspace.

How to use the Impact/Effort Matrix (Eisenhower) template

- Create your task overview. Start by adding all your team's tasks, to-dos, and ideas as sticky notes in the brainstorming space.
- Sort your tasks. Work with your team to drag and drop each sticky note into one of the four quadrants: Urgent & Important (Do first), Less urgent but important (Schedule), Urgent but less important (Delegate), or Neither urgent nor important (Don’t do). Use dot votes to build consensus if there are disagreements.
- Generate an action plan. Once your tasks are sorted, click the “Run” button. Miro AI will automatically read the sticky notes and convert them into a structured action plan in a table format.
- Assign owners. Review the action plan with your team. To assign tasks, simply drag and drop team members’ avatars from the people panel directly onto the items in the table.

Why use the Impact/Effort Matrix (Eisenhower) template?

- Make objective, focused decisions. Move beyond gut feelings and endless debates. This template provides a structured method to evaluate tasks, helping your team focus its energy on the work that matters most.
- Build clear team alignment. The visual matrix makes it easy for everyone to see and understand the team’s priorities at a glance. Collaborative features like dot voting ensure every voice is heard, leading to stronger buy-in.
- Turn discussion into action instantly. Bridge the gap between planning and doing. With a single click, you can use AI to transform your prioritized tasks into a ready-to-use action plan, complete with assignees and deadlines.

About the Scorecard Automator 3000 template

Are you behind on submitting your scorecards? Writing detailed interview feedback is a critical, but often time-consuming, part of the hiring process. The Scorecard Automator 3000 template is a tool designed to turn your team's candidate debrief conversations into clear, structured, and unbiased written summaries. Instead of staring at a blank text box, you can use the power of AI to create a comprehensive, beautiful evaluation that you can copy and paste directly into your hiring platform.

How to use the Scorecard Automator 3000 template

- Conduct Interviews & Schedule Debrief. After conducting all your candidate interviews, schedule a 30-minute debrief meeting with the hiring manager and other interviewers.
- Record Your Discussion. Important: Make sure you record the call. During the meeting, discuss each candidate one by one, covering what you liked and didn't like about each. Once the call is complete, download the transcript.
- Paste Your Transcript & Generate. Simply paste the meeting transcript directly onto the Miro board and activate the AI flow. It will process the conversation and synthesize the discussion into a structured summary for each candidate, following the specific format you provide.
- Finalize Your Scorecard. The template will output a detailed evaluation, ready to be copied into your official scorecard system. This creates a clear record and helps the team make a final hiring decision.

Why use the Scorecard Automator 3000 template?

- Save time on administrative tasks. Drastically reduce the time it takes to write detailed interview feedback, freeing you up to focus on finding the right candidate.
- Create consistent and objective evaluations. The template provides a uniform structure for feedback, ensuring every candidate is evaluated against the same criteria and helping to reduce personal bias.
- Accelerate your hiring process. By making it easier and faster to submit feedback, you can unblock the next steps, get to a hiring decision faster, and avoid losing great candidates.
- Adaptable to your format. Customize the flow to synthesize feedback according to your company's specific scorecard instructions for seamless integration.

It’s designed to make your OKR meetings more effective, collaborative, and forward-moving.

How to use the Weekly OKR Meeting template

- Update your key results. Before the meeting, each team member adds their latest progress to the central OKR tracker. This ensures everyone is working from the same real-time data, including milestones and updates that impacted success metrics.
- Set the agenda. The template helps you identify which key results are at risk or behind, allowing you to build an agenda focused on the most critical topics. You can even use Miro AI to automatically generate an agenda based on the latest data.
- Run the discussion. During the meeting, use the agenda to guide your conversation, focusing on solving problems and removing blockers for the highest-priority items.
- Create an action plan. Capture the outputs of your discussion in the action plan section. Assign owners and deadlines to ensure decisions turn into tangible next steps.

Why use the Weekly OKR Meeting template?

- Focus on what matters most. By updating progress beforehand in the OKR tracker, you can use precious meeting time to discuss at-risk items and make decisions, rather than just sharing status updates.
- Create clear accountability. The template ends with a dedicated action plan table, so everyone leaves the meeting knowing exactly what they need to do next—with clear assignees and due dates to keep goals on track.
- Improve team alignment. With a single, shared view of all OKRs, their status, and the resulting action plan, your team gains a clear and consistent understanding of shared priorities.
